Hero.headline: Natural Code,
Hero.description: Hüma is a high-performance programming language featuring native Turkish syntax. Built with a Rust-backed core, it combines the expressiveness of natural language with the raw speed of a systems compiler.

natural_syntax.title: Natural Syntax
natural_syntax.description: Code using Turkish suffixes that feel natural and improve readability.

hybrid_execution.title: Hybrid Execution
hybrid_execution.description: Mix interpretation for development speed and compilation for production power.

standalone_compilation.title: Standalone Binary
standalone_compilation.description: Compile your code to a single, statically linked binary for any system.

standard_library.title: Standard Library
standard_library.description: Built-in modules for HTTP, GUI, and system management.

CTA.headline: Start Building Now
CTA.description: Join the community of developers building the next generation of software with Hüma.
CTA.available_platforms: Available on Windows, macOS, and Linux

introduction.title: Intro & Philosophy
introduction.description: Discover Hüma's design principles and why it's so fast.
introduction.hero_title: New Wings of Modern Software: Hüma
introduction.hero_desc: Hüma is not just a programming language; it's an ecosystem designed to maximize Developer Experience (DX). It combines the safety of Rust with the natural suffix system of Turkish.

cognitive_ease.title: Cognitive Ease
cognitive_ease.desc: Coding in a natural language structure removes barriers between logical thought and syntax, reducing the margin for error.

agglutination.title: Suffix System
agglutination.desc: Hüma's unique suffix system increases readability while converting into pure performance at compile time.

industrial.title: Industrial Strength
industrial.desc: Unlike loose-typed languages, Hüma provides a robust infrastructure that can be used safely in critical systems.

why_huma.title: Why Hüma?
points: Safety Built on Top of Rust

syntax.title: Basic Syntax
syntax.description: Variables, data types, and Hüma's unique suffix system.
syntax.hero_desc: Hüma's syntax offers logical clarity and flexibility. In this section, we'll examine variable definition, data types, and the heart of the language: the 'Suffix System'.

variables.title: Variable Definition
variables.desc: In Hüma, everything is a value. You can define both local and global variables with the 'olsun' keyword.

suffix_system.title: Suffix System
suffix_system.desc: Hüma supports Turkish's agglutinative structure at the compiler level. All suffixes following the apostrophe (') are automatically stripped by the compiler, achieving both natural language and high performance.

functions_classes.title: Functions & Classes
functions_classes.description: Modern object-oriented programming and functional structures.
functions_classes.hero_desc: Hüma supports both functional and object-oriented programming paradigms. In this section, you'll learn how to make your code modular and how to build complex data structures.

functions.title: Functions
functions.desc: Functions are the fundamental building blocks of Hüma. You can easily manage data flow with 'alsın' and 'döndür' structures.

classes.title: Classes (Object-Oriented)
classes.desc: Classes in Hüma are powerful structures that hold data and behavior together. You can access object properties with the 'bu' (self) keyword.

methods.title: Methods & 'bu' Call
methods.desc: Inside a method belonging to an object, the object itself is accessed with the 'bu' keyword. This is the cornerstone of object-oriented programming.

lists_errors.title: Lists & Errors
lists_errors.description: Advanced data structures and safe error handling techniques.
lists_errors.hero_desc: Hüma offers advanced tools for managing your application's data flow and handling unexpected situations. Handle data sets with lists and prevent crashes with the 'hata' structure.

lists.title: Working with Lists
lists.desc: Hüma lists are dynamic and come with various helper functions. Manage your data easily with functions like 'ekle', 'çıkar', and 'boyut'.

error_handling.title: Error Handling
error_handling.desc: Provides a safe exit strategy for bottlenecks in your programs. You can exit a process in a controlled way or inform upper layers with the 'hata' keyword.

stdlib.title: Standard Library
stdlib.description: Hüma's built-in API reference and helper modules.
stdlib.hero_desc: Hüma comes with a rich set of built-in libraries to speed up your development process. Everything from mathematics to the file system is optimized.
stdlib.import_info: Use the '@kullan' directive to include a module in your project:

modules.renkler: ANSI color outputs for current terminals.
modules.dosya: File read, write, and existence checks.

compiler.title: Compiler Internals
compiler.description: Deep-dive into the Hüma compiler pipeline: lexer, suffix system, AST, and interpreter.
compiler.hero_desc: Hüma is a high-performance interpreter powered by Rust. Your source code is first tokenized, then transformed into a syntax tree, and finally executed by traversing the tree.

pipeline.title: The Pipeline
pipeline.desc: Hüma source code passes through four main stages: Source Text, Lexer (Tokenizer), Parser, and Interpreter.

lexer.title: Lexer & Suffix System
lexer.desc: The Hüma lexer strips Turkish grammatical suffixes (i'yi, yı, nı etc.) at compile time to determine the root word. This process has zero runtime cost.

ast.title: Abstract Syntax Tree (AST)
ast.desc: The parser transforms the token stream into a tree of Rust enums. Each node represents a language construct: statements, expressions, and blocks.

build.title: Build from Source
build.desc: To build the Hüma compiler on your machine, a stable Rust toolchain (rustup) is required.

network.title: Network & Web
network.description: HTTP client (ag_istekleri) and high-performance web server (huma_sunucu).
network.hero_desc: Hüma can communicate with the web world as both a client and a server. In this section, you'll learn how to consume APIs with ag_istekleri and build your own services with huma_sunucu.

requests.title: Network Requests (Client)
requests.desc: The ag_istekleri library supports modern HTTP protocols (GET, POST, PUT, DELETE). v1.1.0 introduces Headers support, enabling authenticated (Bearer/Token) API communication.

server.title: Hüma Server (v1.4.0)
server.desc: huma_sunucu is a low-latency, high-performance backend framework. Dynamic route parameters, CORS management, and static file serving come built-in.

gui.title: Graphical User Interface (GUI)
gui.hero_desc: Hüma provides a native GUI layer based on Rust's egui library for developing modern desktop applications. With this library, you can create windows, add buttons, text boxes, and complex layouts.

quick_start.title: Quick Start
quick_start.desc: Define your main window with the 'pencere_oluştur' function and build the interface with a drawing callback function.

package_manager.title: Package Manager
package_manager.description: Manage, update, and download Hüma libraries from the community.
package_manager.hero_desc: Hüma Package Manager simplifies library management for your projects. You can manage your dependencies securely with version locking using 'huma.json' and 'huma.lock' files.
package_manager.manifest_desc: Every Hüma package must contain a 'huma.json' file in its root directory. This file defines the package name, version, and dependencies.
package_manager.dependencies_desc: Hüma supports installing dependencies from both GitHub and local directories. The huma.lock file ensures that your project uses the same dependency versions in every installation.
package_manager.publishing_desc: To publish your own package, simply upload the code to GitHub and add a valid SemVer tag (like v1.0.0).
package_manager.scripts_desc: You can define custom automation scripts in 'huma.json' just like in npm. Run them using 'huma paket run <name>'.
package_manager.verify_desc: Automatically verify if your package metadata and versioning are compliant before sharing.

platform.title: Official Sharing Platform
platform.desc: Browse hundreds of ready-to-use libraries and share your own solutions with the community.

my_first_package.title: My First Package
my_first_package.description: A step-by-step guide to creating a Hüma library and publishing it via GitHub.
my_first_package.hero_desc: Contribute to the Hüma community by creating and sharing your own libraries. This guide will show you how to build a package from scratch.

ide.title: Hüma IDE
ide.description: Official Hüma desktop editor and development environment.
ide.hero_desc: Hüma comes with its own lightweight desktop editor to provide a first-class developer experience. This modern structure based on Tauri and React targets performance and ease of use.

architecture.title: Architectural Structure
architecture.desc: The IDE provides a secure and fast desktop experience by combining a modern web stack (Vite+React) with the power of Rust (Tauri).

changelog.title: Changelog
changelog.description: Official update history of the Hüma language and toolchain.
changelog.hero_desc: Hüma is constantly evolving. In every version, you can track new language features, performance improvements, and community-driven bug fixes here.

changes: Modern Networking: added high-performance HTTP client via ag_istekleri library.
Huma Server (HTTP): built-in web server framework with dynamic route management.
Bilingual CLI: all commands are now usable in both Turkish and English.
Byte Data Type: built-in support for processing images, audio and other binary files.
Package Manager 2.0: script support, integrity checks and automated git integration.
Server Response Headers: object-based header support for customizing HTTP responses.
Native Graphical User Interface (GUI) library (gui.hb) launched based on egui.
Launched the 'Hüma Kütüphaneleri' community sharing platform.
Added high-performance web server support with the new 'huma_sunucu' core module.
Advanced Package Manager (huma paket) and GitHub integration added.
huma.lock lockfile system implemented for reproducible builds.
SemVer compatible version control and package metadata (huma.json) introduced.
Full Bilingual CLI support (e.g., 'run' or 'çalıştır', 'build' or 'derle').
npm-style package scripts support with 'baslat'/'start' preference.
Optimized syntax and drawing loops for GUI modules.

getting_started.title: Getting Started
getting_started.description: Install Hüma on your system and write your first high-performance program in minutes.
getting_started.hero_desc: Welcome to the Hüma ecosystem — a high-performance language designed for precision and kinetic speed. This guide will help you optimize your environment and write your first professional Hüma program.

installation.title: Installation
installation.desc: The Hüma toolchain natively supports Windows, Linux, and macOS (x64/ARM64) architectures. Our installation script automatically detects your system's hardware.
installation.arch_desc: Hüma runs with zero latency on Apple Silicon (M1/M2/M3) and modern x86_64 processors. The compiler core is built using safe, high-performance Rust.

quick_start.title: Verification & REPL
quick_start.desc: Verify your installation and start experimenting immediately in the interactive shell (REPL).

first_project.title: Your First Project
first_project.desc: Hüma's package manager creates a professional project structure with just one command. This sets up your manifest, boilerplate, and dependency management.
first_project.suffix_desc: The 'huma paket yeni' command automatically generates a 'huma.json' file and a main entry point, preparing your project for scale from day one.

syntax.title: Basic Syntax
syntax.desc: Variables, Operators, and the Suffix System

lists.title: Data & Errors
lists.desc: Advanced Data Structures and Error Handling

Playground.headline: Meet Hüma,
Playground.description: Experience the power and simplicity of Hüma through our ready-to-use examples. See how the code works without any installation required.
Playground.cta_desc: Follow the documentation to start high-performance Hüma development on your local machine.

hello.label: Hello World
hello.description: Variable definition and basic output operations.

functions.label: Functions
functions.description: Modular structures that can take parameters and return values.
